M-065.NAME: mtproto-cta-subdomain-link-generation
M-065.path: backend/app/mtproto/provisioning.py backend/app/mtproto/service.py backend/tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py backend/tests/test_deploy_phase38_static.py frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx deploy/haproxy-phase38.cfg scripts/phase38-de-mtproto-edge-smoke.mjs scripts/phase47-mtproto-cta-links-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-47.xml
M-065.owns: CTA-prefixed MTProto SNI generation, public short user identifier derivation, explicit prefix validation, and owner link compatibility for Telegram app/browser links.
M-065.purpose: Generate readable marketing-oriented personal MTProto proxy hostnames that encourage visits to krotpn.xyz without breaking issued-link availability or exposing raw internal user IDs.

exports.const-mtproto-cta-prefixes: Allowed transliterated CTA prefixes: kupi-vpn, vpn-tut, beri-vpn, bez-blokirovok, hochu-bystree, krot-vpn.
exports.fn-shorten-public-user-id: Derive a 4-hex public suffix from a hex/UUID-like user key or from a stable hash when the stored user_id is numeric.
exports.fn-select-cta-prefix: Choose either an explicit validated CTA prefix or a stable pseudo-random prefix for rotation-safe provisioning.
exports.fn-generate-cta-sni: Build wildcard-safe SNI in the format cta-prefix-shortid.base-domain with collision nonce support.
exports.fn-issue-cta-proxy-payload: Return owner-safe MTProto payloads whose server, secret, tg link, and browser link use the final CTA SNI.
exports.ru-sni-router-cta-compatibility: Ensure new approved CTA-prefix-4hex and existing CTA-prefix-7hex hostnames match the RU SNI router MTProto ACL and do not fall back to the HTTPS backend.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not change install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, wildcard TLS issuance, database schema, or RU/DE topology in Phase-47. The post-release hotfix permits only the HAProxy SNI ACL compatibility update needed to route CTA labels.
constraints.constraint-2: Generated SNI must remain exactly one wildcard label under the configured base domain so existing *.krotpn.xyz TLS and RU SNI routing continue to work.
constraints.constraint-3: Numeric internal user IDs must not be exposed directly in public hostnames; use a stable 4-hex public suffix instead.
constraints.constraint-4: Explicit CTA prefix input must be restricted to the approved allow-list and never accept arbitrary DNS labels.
constraints.constraint-5: Existing active u-*.krotpn.xyz assignments must remain valid unless an explicit reissue path is requested.
constraints.constraint-6: Derived fake-TLS secret must be computed from the final CTA SNI, and no base secret, salt, derived secret, or full proxy link may be logged.

acceptance-metrics.metric-1: New assignment hostnames follow cta-prefix-4hex.krotpn.xyz and stay below DNS label limits.
acceptance-metrics.metric-2: Allowed explicit prefix and stable pseudo-random prefix selection are deterministic and covered by tests.
acceptance-metrics.metric-3: Numeric IDs are transformed to a non-raw 4-hex public suffix while hex-like IDs can use their first 4 hex characters.
acceptance-metrics.metric-4: Owner payload server, secret, tg:// link, and https://t.me link all reference the same CTA SNI.
acceptance-metrics.metric-5: Backend tests, frontend build, Phase-38/47 smoke, MyGRACE lint, XML parse, and deploy/install diff guard pass.
acceptance-metrics.metric-6: RU SNI router routes legacy u-12hex, existing CTA-prefix-7hex, and new CTA-prefix-4hex issued hostnames to mtproto_de_runtime while unknown SNI stays on HTTPS fallback.

verification-design.evidence-level-1: Module-local backend tests verify prefix allow-list, short-id derivation, SNI validation, fake-TLS secret binding, and legacy assignment preservation.
verification-design.evidence-level-2: Wave-level frontend and smoke checks verify owner payload tg:// and https://t.me links use the same CTA server without exposing real secret-bearing links.
verification-design.evidence-level-3: Phase-level static guards verify no install/deploy-script/nginx/docker-compose/runtime topology or wildcard TLS surfaces changed, and the HAProxy router ACL accepts approved CTA labels.

execution-evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-01: backend/app/mtproto/provisioning.py now exposes MTPROTO_CTA_PREFIXES, shorten_public_user_id, select_cta_prefix, and generate_cta_sni.
execution-evidence.evidence-2: 2026-06-01: new assignment issuance uses CTA SNI while active legacy u-* assignments are reused without automatic mass reissue.
execution-evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-01: backend tests passed 14/14, frontend build passed, Phase-47 smoke passed, XML parse passed, MyGRACE lint passed, and protected deploy surfaces had no diff.
execution-evidence.evidence-4: 2026-06-01: Hotfix closed live operator report for unavailable CTA links by changing HAProxy MTProto ACL from legacy-only u-12hex to legacy-or-approved-CTA-prefix-7hex, then passing Phase-38, Phase-41, Phase-47, and deploy static verification locally.
execution-evidence.evidence-5: 2026-06-01: Short-suffix fix changed new CTA assignments from 7-hex to 4-hex public suffixes for Telegram UI readability while keeping HAProxy compatible with existing 7-hex CTA links.

M-065.hotspot: H-052: CTA MTProto SNI generation must stay wildcard-safe, avoid exposing raw numeric user IDs, preserve old issued links, and not weaken RU/DE routing
